metadata.focus: Mature B2B/B2C markets with digital gaps
metadata.note: All data is March 2026 snapshot, regions vary

niches.name: Funeral Services Aggregator (Funerarias)
pain_points: Zero online presence (1100 companies, ~5% have modern website)
No price comparison (customers pay 2-3x premium due to information vacuum)

mvp.description: Funeral service comparison + online booking + Bizum payment integration
core_features: Display: photos, hours, services, prices, reviews (auto-import from Google)

sales_strategy.phase_1: Cold calls + WhatsApp to funeral homes (target: free 1-2 months to get listings)
sales_strategy.phase_2: Google Ads (low CPC due to zero competition)

niches.regulatory_notes: Funeral services not strictly regulated for aggregators (platform = marketplace, not provider). GDPR standard.

niches.name: Digital Notary Portal (Notarios SaaS)

regulatory_context.allowed_services: Digital notarization, videoconference signing, remote acts

current_solutions.problem: All 20+ years old, no modern client portal

pain_points: No client portal (customers must visit office to check status)
No bank integration (payment separate from service)
No analytics (don't know which services are profitable)
Videoconference allowed by law, but workflows not designed for it

mvp.description: Cloud-based client portal + admin dashboard for notaries
core_features: Client portal: transaction status, document access, e-signature (Docusign API)
Notary admin: queue management, document archive, payment tracking
Video conference integration: scheduled calls, recording

sales_strategy.phase_1: Warm introductions (leverage notary associations, regional colleges)
sales_strategy.phase_2: Geographic: target 1 city/region, get critical mass
sales_strategy.leverage: Once 5 notaries in city adopt → others request features = viral

niches.competitive_advantage: First modern portal = first to capture network effect in city

niches.name: Vet Clinic + Pet Insurance Integration (Veterinarias)

current_solutions.gap: None of them integrate with pet insurance (pre-auth, claims submission)

pain_points: No pre-authorization (vet doesn't know what insurance covers)
Manual claims submission (clinic staff must call insurer, fax docs)
Payment delay (clinic gets reimbursed in 2-4 weeks)
Fraud risk (insurers can't verify claim details)
Customer confusion ("Will it be covered?") = lost sales

mvp.description: Plugin/integration for QVET and WinVet: pre-auth + automated claims
core_features: Pre-auth: vet enters procedure → system queries insurer → approval/denial in real-time
Claims submission: receipt + diagnosis + invoice auto-submitted to insurer
Dashboard for insurer: claims queue, approve/deny, instant Bizum reimbursement
Customer visibility: what's covered (in clinic + app)
Integration: QVET API, WinVet API, insurer APIs

sales_strategy.phase_1: Partner with 1 insurer (e.g., Adeslas) - get them to mandate integration
sales_strategy.phase_2: Clinic adoption (insurer recommends it as free add-on)
sales_strategy.phase_3: Scale to other insurers (ASISA, Sanitas, Línea Directa)

market_dynamics.driver_2: Insurers need tech to reduce fraud

niches.competitive_advantage: First to integrate = de facto standard in Spain for 3+ years

runner_ups.name: Auto Dealer EV Trade-in AI Valuation
runner_ups.reason: High CPC (€2.5+), real pain, but Cazoo/Vroom competition incoming in 12-18 months

runner_ups.name: Fitness Club AI Personal Trainer
runner_ups.reason: Large market, but Freeletics/Future dominate. Integration complexity high.

not_recommended.name: Opticians Software (Ópticas SaaS)
not_recommended.reason: High competition (Gesvision, Optiplus), low margins, independents resist change

summary.key_finding: Funeral aggregator is most underserved market in Spain (0 competitors, €1.5B TAM, 60% willingness to pay)
summary.timing: 2026 = window closing (Cazoo/Vroom EU expansion, international players waking up)
summary.risk_level: Low (proven business model in UK/US, just missing in Spain)
summary.team_size: 1-2 person MVP possible for all three
summary.next_action: Pick ONE. Run 5-10 customer calls. Validate willingness to pay. Build MVP in 6-8 weeks.
